clinical_history: What intermittent arrhythmia do you think this 76-year-old woman was recently treated for with a drug? There are two major ECG clues.
diagnosis:
Atrial Fibrillation. The rhythm is now sinus, with borderline sinus bradyardia, at about 60/min
Note: 1) The wide notched P wave in lead II and biphasic P wave in lead V1 are consistent with left atrial abnormality (LAA). The initial component of the P wave is slightly peaked in lead V1, also raising consideration of biatrial abnormality. However, this finding is not present in the limb leads.
         2) The long QT/QTc (about 0.47 second), consistent with amiodarone effect, a commony used antiarrhythmic drug for paroxymal atrial fibrillation in this age-group. This drug may also cause sinus bradycardia related to beta-blocking effects. What are other effects and potential toxicities of this drug?
